name: Baked Chimichangas
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the tortillas.
2. Prepare Almond Red Sauce and Jalepeno Cream Sauce; reserve.
3. Cook ground beef, onion, and garlic in a skillet over medium heat until brown. Drain excess fat.
4. Stir in remaining ingredients except tortillas, egg, and margarine. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Preheat oven to 500°F (260°C).
6. Spoon about half a cup of beef mixture onto the center of each tortilla.
7. Fold one end of the tortilla up about an inch over the beef mixture. Fold right side over folded edge and lift sides. Fold remaining end down.
8. Brush edges with beaten egg to seal, then brush each chimichanga with softened margarine.
9. Place seam sides down in a greased jelly roll pan (15 ½ x 10 ½ x 1 inch).
10. Bake until tortillas begin to brown and filling is hot, about 8-10 minutes.
11. Serve with Almond Red Sauce and Jalepeno Cream Sauce.
FRIED CHIMICHANGAS:
Omit the softened margarine.
Heat vegetable oil (about 1 inch) to 365°F (185°C).
Fry chimichangas in batches until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes. Keep warm at 300°F (150°C).
